sc-04.title: The Spectroscope and the Empty Plate

sc-10a.title: Confront: The Telegram He Believes In

sc-11a.title: Confront: The Letter That Will Not Agree

sc-11b.title: Audit: The Key in the Lining

sc-12a.title: Confront: What Pike Confesses and What He Cannot

sc-12b.title: Audit: The Shape of the Theft

sc-17r.title: Reveal: The Shape Against the Shutter

sc-17w.title: Burn: The Log Goes Into the Stove

sc-18b.title: Audit: The Pier and the Hour

sc-18r.title: Reveal: The Key That Was Never Lost

sc-19b.title: Audit: The Climber Reasoned, Not Seen

hale.description: Lewin Hale's working theory as the adjuster reasons it out

pike.description: Onslow Pike's false belief — that Crane knew of his dismissal

audit.description: Fork-1 limb: Hale quietly audits the ledgers and watches

burn.description: Fork-2 limb: Junia burns the night-log to bury the truth (continues confront limb)

confront.description: Fork-1 limb: Hale confronts Pike at sc-08

reveal.description: Fork-2 limb: Junia reveals what she saw on the gallery (continues confront limb)

climber.description: the unknown figure Junia saw climb the iron stair at 3:14 (identity is the central mystery)

dome-key.description: the spare dome key reported lost

plate-camera.description: the plate-camera and its blank plate

telegram.description: the forged patron's telegram of recall

identity.description: the real identity of an otherwise-unnamed figure

key-custody.description: who holds the spare dome key

knows-dismissal.description: whether Crane knows he was recalled to dismissal

location.description: where a character physically is during the death-night

gt-account-discrepancy-surfaced.claim: At sc-08 the instrument account's missing sum becomes a hard discrepancy on the page that Hale cannot un-see: the motive is now on the table.

gt-account-drained.claim: Pike has quietly drained money from the instrument account; the forged recall was meant to remove Crane before the loss was found.

gt-audit-certifies.claim: Building the whole case from objects and never a confession, Hale certifies the fraud against Pike and rules the death an intrusion-driven fall — Pike entered unseen with the kept key, the frail director was startled and fell — and the policy does not pay a clean accident.

gt-audit-clock-pier.claim: Tracing the bolts to the shared north pier, Hale reads the stopped clock at last: nothing told it to stop — the fall rang the pier and stilled the brittle pendulum, fixing 3:14 as the recorded instant Crane went down.

gt-audit-letter-read.claim: Hale sets the unsent resignation beside the undelivered wire and sees Crane meant to lay down his post of his own grief over his failing eyes — no one pushed him; the old astronomer was already walking toward a door he intended to open himself.

gt-audit-log-reasoned.claim: Hale reasons the strange hand without forcing it from Junia: the log is her writing because Crane's cold-stiffened fingers could no longer form fine letters and she took down his observations at his elbow — the second hand was a kindness, not a forger's mark.

gt-audit-plate-blank.claim: On the audit limb Hale develops the racked plate himself, alone, and finds it blank — no exposure made — the silent physical proof that Junia was never at the spectroscope, exposing her alibi-lie without a single confession.

gt-audit-reconstructs.claim: Saying nothing, Hale reconstructs the whole theft from the books alone — every spooned guinea, every phantom freight — and lets Pike believe himself unsuspected.

gt-audit-telegram-found.claim: In the loose papers Pike handed over, Hale finds the copy-book draft of the recall and traces the wire to the undelivered box at the foot of the road — the forged telegram in Pike's hand that never reached Crane, proving the dismissal the bursar built his guilt upon was never read.

gt-burn-certifies.claim: With the log burned and Junia silent, Hale cannot prove the second hand was innocent or name who climbed; he certifies the fraud against Pike but must rule the death itself accidental and unproven — the policy pays, ironically, because Junia destroyed the proof.

gt-burn-key-inferred.claim: Without the log Hale cannot name the climber, but he can still read the key: the spare reported lost a month before, in the proven thief's hand, was the means by which someone entered the dome unseen — the one piece of the night he can certify even with the page burned.

gt-burn-log-destroyed.claim: Junia feeds the night-log to the workroom stove, destroying the one hard record of the hour and the hand, and keeps the climber's name beyond Hale's reach for good.

gt-climber-pike.claim: The man Junia saw climb the iron stair at 3:14 was Onslow Pike; he let himself in by the lower dome door with the spare key to take the duplicate ledgers from Crane's safe, not to kill — but the blind director, already on the dark stair over his own grief, was startled and fell.

gt-clock-jolt.claim: The meridian clock, wound only hours before, stopped at 3:14 when the fall jolted the iron stair whose frame shares the clock's pendulum case.

gt-clock-read-true.claim: Tracing the bolts, Hale sees the clock and the iron stair share one north pier; a heavy fall would ring the pier and still the brittle pendulum — the clock was jolted, not set.

gt-crane-dead.claim: Dr. Aurel Crane is dead at the foot of the iron stair, having fallen from the top in the dark.
gt-crane-dead.quote: found dead at the foot of the iron stair beneath the great refractor

gt-hale-sees-key.claim: From the dark of the stair-head Hale watches Pike draw the lost spare key from his coat and let himself into the lower dome door — the key that was reported lost a month before.

gt-junia-cornered.claim: Cornered by the blank plate, Junia must choose: tell Hale what she saw on the gallery, or destroy the night-log and let the summit keep its dead man's secret.

gt-junia-gallery.claim: Junia Frost was on the dome gallery that night, not at the spectroscope, and she saw who climbed the iron stair.

gt-junia-lies.claim: Junia lies that she was at the spectroscope until dawn, to shield the man she saw climb the stair.

gt-key-found.claim: The spare dome key, reported lost a month before, was taken and hidden by Pike; it lets him into the dome unseen.

gt-letter-private.claim: Crane had written an unsent letter resigning his own post, a private despair over his failing eyesight, unconnected to Pike's plot.

gt-letter-resolves.claim: The unsent resignation, read against the undelivered telegram, shows Crane meant to leave quietly of his own grief — no one drove him to the stair.

gt-log-junia-hand.claim: The night-log is in Junia's hand because Crane's cold-stiffened hands could not write; she recorded the observation for him, so the strange hand is innocent.

gt-log-truth-surfaces.claim: Pressed, Junia admits the night-log is her hand because she wrote at Crane's dictation, his fingers being too stiffened to write — the strange hand was innocent all along.

gt-pike-buries.claim: Believing himself a near-murderer who must hide every thread, Pike moves at night to bury the duplicate ledgers and the spare dome key still warm in his coat-lining.

gt-pike-confesses.claim: Confronted with the open ledger at sc-09a, Pike breaks and confesses the drained account and the forged telegram, believing he is confessing to a death he caused.

gt-pike-forged.claim: Onslow Pike forged the patron's telegram recalling Crane to dismissal, to cover the fund he drained from the instrument account.

gt-plate-empty.claim: The plate-camera holds no exposures because Junia never stood at the spectroscope that night; the blank plate is the physical contradiction of her alibi.

gt-plate-pressed.claim: Hale at last develops the racked plate himself and finds it blank — no star drawn, no exposure made — the physical proof that Junia was not at the spectroscope that night.

gt-reveal-certifies.claim: Hale certifies the death not the murder Pike confessed to but a fall caused by Pike's unlawful intrusion upon a frail man — the policy does not pay a clean accident; the fraud and forgery stand exposed, and Pike answers for the theft, not the killing he believes he did.

gt-reveal-key-explained.claim: With Pike named, the lost spare key finds its place: it was never lost but kept in Pike's coat, the hinge that let him into the dome unseen the night Crane fell — the means the whole intrusion swung upon.

gt-reveal-names-pike.claim: Junia names the shape on the stair at last: Onslow Pike, climbing in the dark with the spare key; she has shielded him a fortnight because she knew the fall was no murder and would not see a thief hanged for a death he did not mean.

gt-telegram-found-undelivered.claim: Hale traces the recall wire to the post-box at the foot of the road and finds it undelivered, proving Crane never read it and never knew of any dismissal.

gt-telegram-undelivered.claim: The forged telegram never reached Crane; it lay undelivered in the post-box at the foot of the hill, so Crane never knew he was to be dismissed.

hale-accident.claim: Hale's working theory: Crane rose alone in the night, slipped on the frozen stair, and fell — an ordinary accident the policy would pay.

hale-audit-plate-suspect.claim: The blank plate turns Junia from witness to liar in Hale's book: she was somewhere else in the dome that night, and her steady alibi becomes the lie he failed to hear.

hale-audit-reasons-truth.claim: Hale assembles the truth without a single confession: the clock records the instant of the fall, the key proves Pike's unseen entry, the wire proves Crane never knew — a death by intrusion and frailty, not the murder a confession would have named.

hale-burn-thwarted.claim: Hale watches the log burn and knows the case has closed against him: he holds the fraud whole but the death has slipped past proof, and the climber's name is gone to ash.

hale-clock-jolt-grasped.claim: Hale abandons the told-clock theory: 3:14 is not a planted alibi but the recorded instant of the fall itself, fixing when Crane went down the stair.

hale-doubt-confront.claim: Holding the undelivered telegram and the private letter, Hale doubts his own killer-theory: if Crane never knew, the confrontation Pike confesses to never reached him, and the fall is unexplained again.

hale-junia-credible.claim: Hale takes Junia at her word that she worked at the spectroscope until dawn, and treats her as a witness rather than a suspect.

hale-junia-suspect.claim: The blank plate destroys Junia's alibi for Hale: she was somewhere else in the dome that night, and her steady account becomes the lie he failed to hear.

hale-knows-junia-lied.claim: Hale now knows Junia lied about the spectroscope, knows she was elsewhere in the dome, and faces her at sc-16 certain she saw what he cannot: who climbed the stair.

hale-log-innocent.claim: Hale accepts the night-log was no forger's mark but a scribe's kindness, and drops the second-person-in-the-dome reading the strange hand had carried.

hale-log-suspect.claim: Hale reads the night-log's strange hand as a sign of a second person in the dome — possibly a forger, possibly the killer.

hale-pike-intruder.claim: Watching Pike use the hidden key, Hale's theory firms into intrusion: the bursar entered the dome unseen the night of the death, and the accident was no accident.

hale-pike-killer.claim: After the confession, Hale's theory hardens: the bursar drove the director to the stair and is morally, perhaps criminally, the cause of his death.

hale-reveal-truth.claim: Hale's theory completes: Junia's witness, the empty plate, the jolted clock, the hidden key and the undelivered wire assemble into one true account — Pike intruded, Crane fell, no one planned a death.

pike-confession-belief.claim: Pike confesses not to theft alone but to a killing he is sure of: he believes his forged recall reached Crane and put the ruined man at the rail in the dark.

pike-crane-knew.claim: Pike believes Crane received the recall and knew he was to be dismissed in disgrace — and that this is why Crane was on the stair at three in the morning.

pike-guilt.claim: Pike believes the confrontation he forced caused Crane's fall, and carries himself as a near-murderer who must bury the ledgers and the key.

dc-v2.description: v2 gate-validation telling: default withhold; the two load-bearing solution conclusions revealed at their per-world reveal scenes
